name: Airtable MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that gives AI systems full read/write access to Airtable bases, including record CRUD, schema management, field creation, and record comments.
use_cases: Query and update Airtable records via natural language through an AI assistant
Automate Airtable schema management (create tables, add fields)
Search across Airtable records with full-text search
Add comments to Airtable records programmatically
Build AI-powered workflows on top of Airtable data
not_for: Replacing Airtable's native automations for high-volume batch operations
Real-time sync or webhook-driven workflows
Working with non-Airtable databases
best_when: You use Airtable as a data backend and want an AI assistant to read, write, and manage your bases through natural language.
avoid_when: You need high-throughput batch operations, real-time sync, or are working with databases other than Airtable.

auth.notes: Requires Airtable Personal Access Token (PAT) via AIRTABLE_API_KEY env var. Tokens scoped per-base with granular permissions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Passes through Airtable API error messages; generally informative
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record reads are idempotent; writes are not. No built-in deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP transport mode has NO built-in authentication - must be behind a reverse proxy
Token scopes must be configured per-base, easy to miss required permissions
Airtable rate limit of 5 req/sec per base can throttle busy agents
delete_records is destructive with no undo - agents should confirm before deletion

security_score_components.security_notes: Scoped PAT tokens are a security positive. HTTP transport mode lacks auth - a significant risk if exposed. HTTPS enforced to Airtable API. Secret via env var.

name: Akamai API
what_it_does: Enterprise CDN and cloud security platform API for managing content delivery, WAF rules, DDoS mitigation, bot management, and edge application delivery policies.
use_cases: Programmatic cache invalidation (fast purge) from deployment pipelines
Managing WAF security policies and firewall rules via Akamai OPEN APIs
Configuring property rules and edge behaviors for traffic management
Retrieving CDN traffic analytics and real-time reporting
Bot Manager configuration and bot traffic analysis
not_for: Small websites or apps not requiring enterprise CDN pricing
Simple static site hosting
Teams without Akamai contracts
best_when: You're an enterprise customer using Akamai CDN and need programmatic control over delivery configurations, security policies, and cache operations.
avoid_when: You're not an Akamai customer - Fastly or Cloudflare have more developer-friendly on-ramps.

auth.notes: Akamai EdgeGrid signing - custom HMAC-based request signing. Credentials scoped to specific API access.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP errors with descriptive messages; EdgeGrid auth failures can be cryptic
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Cache purge is idempotent. Property activation is versioned and idempotent per version.
known_agent_gotchas: EdgeGrid authentication is unique to Akamai - must use Akamai SDK or EdgeGrid signing library
Property Manager uses a version/activation model - changes require creating a new version and activating it
40+ separate API families - must know which API handles which feature
Staging environment activations required before production
Edge behaviors use complex rule tree JSON structure

security_score_components.security_notes: EdgeGrid HMAC signing is more secure than API keys. Scoped credentials per API family. FedRAMP authorized. PCI-DSS compliant. Enterprise-grade security posture.

name: Alchemy API
what_it_does: Blockchain infrastructure platform providing enhanced Ethereum/EVM node RPC access, NFT APIs, token APIs, and real-time event webhooks with best-in-class reliability and developer experience.
use_cases: Querying on-chain data with enhanced APIs beyond standard JSON-RPC
Monitoring wallet activity and smart contract events via webhooks
Fetching NFT metadata and ownership data for collections
Building blockchain agents that read token balances and transfer history
Simulating transactions before sending to detect failures
not_for: Non-EVM blockchains outside Alchemy's supported networks
Private blockchain or permissioned network access
Very high-frequency trading requiring sub-millisecond RPC latency
best_when: You're building on Ethereum/EVM chains and need reliable node infrastructure plus enhanced NFT/token APIs beyond raw RPC.
avoid_when: You're working on Solana, Cosmos, or other non-EVM chains not supported by Alchemy.

auth.notes: API key embedded in the RPC URL endpoint. Alchemy Dashboard allows restricting keys by domain and allowed methods.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC error codes plus Alchemy-specific error extensions; well-documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read methods are idempotent. Transaction submission is not.
known_agent_gotchas: Compute Unit costs vary by method - getNFTs costs 10x more CUs than eth_blockNumber
Different endpoint URLs needed per network (mainnet, testnet, L2s)
WebSocket connections require separate WSS URLs and reconnection logic
Transaction simulation is not 100% accurate for all smart contract interactions
NFT API responses include spam/suspicious NFTs by default - use excludeFilters

security_score_components.security_notes: API key embedded in URL path is a security concern - keys can appear in server access logs. Dashboard-level domain restrictions help. SOC2 certified. No granular scopes.

name: Algolia API
what_it_does: Hosted search platform providing fast, typo-tolerant full-text search with AI-powered relevance, faceting, and filtering, with an official MCP server for agent-driven search integration.
use_cases: Adding instant search to web/mobile apps with AI relevance via MCP server
Ecommerce product search with faceting and merchandising rules
Indexing and querying structured content from agent workflows
Building autocomplete and query suggestions
Neural search combining semantic and keyword matching
not_for: Log analytics or time-series search (use Elasticsearch)
Full document storage (Algolia is a search index, not a database)
Teams with very tight budgets at scale
best_when: You need hosted search with excellent developer experience, fast performance, and AI-powered relevance without managing Elasticsearch infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need log analytics, full-text document storage, or have complex aggregation needs.

auth.notes: Admin API key for write operations. Search-only API key for client-side queries. Application ID + API key pair.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent structured error responses with status, message, and error type
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Index saves are idempotent by objectID. Search is read-only. Async taskID required for consistency.
known_agent_gotchas: Admin API key must never be exposed client-side
Index names are permanent and can't be renamed (only copied)
Indexing is asynchronous - taskID must be waited on
Neural/AI search features require separate configuration and billing
Free tier record limit (10k) hit quickly with product catalogs

security_score_components.security_notes: Good separation of admin vs search-only keys. HIPAA, SOC2, ISO27001 certified. Keys passed in headers. No fine-grained scopes beyond read/write distinction.

name: Alpaca MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Alpaca MCP server enabling AI assistants to trade stocks, ETFs, crypto, and options, manage portfolios, access real-time and historical market data, and handle watchlists through natural language.
use_cases: Place and manage stock, ETF, crypto, and options orders via AI assistant
Monitor account balances, positions, and buying power
Retrieve real-time quotes, historical bars, and options Greeks
Manage watchlists and track corporate actions
Build AI-powered trading workflows with paper or live accounts
not_for: Backtesting historical strategies
Non-Alpaca brokerage accounts
High-frequency or ultra-low-latency trading
best_when: You have an Alpaca brokerage account and want an AI assistant to execute trades and monitor positions through natural language.
avoid_when: You need multi-broker support, backtesting capabilities, or are not comfortable giving an AI agent access to real trading endpoints.

auth.notes: Requires ALPACA_API_KEY and ALPACA_SECRET_KEY env vars. ALPACA_PAPER_TRADE env var toggles between paper and live mode.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Order rejection reasons and API errors passed through; some errors need interpretation
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CRITICAL: Order placement is NOT idempotent - retries create duplicate orders with real financial consequences
known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: Order placement is not idempotent - retries can create duplicate orders with real financial consequences
Paper/live toggle is a single env var - misconfiguration could execute real trades
close_all_positions is extremely destructive and should never be auto-invoked by agents
Options trading (multi-leg) is complex and errors could be costly
No built-in confirmation step before trade execution

security_score_components.security_notes: FINRA/SEC regulated broker but MCP has no scope granularity - single API key grants full trading access. Paper/live toggle via env var is a configuration risk. No confirmation mechanism before trade execution.

name: Alpaca Trading API
what_it_does: REST API for commission-free stock, ETF, and crypto trading, including market data, account management, and order management for algorithmic and agent-based trading strategies.
use_cases: Algorithmic trading with programmatic order placement and portfolio management
Real-time market data streaming for agent decision-making
Paper trading for AI strategy testing before live deployment
Account balance, position, and order history monitoring
Fractional share trading for dollar-cost averaging strategies
not_for: Non-Alpaca brokerage accounts
Complex multi-leg options strategies requiring sophisticated execution
Ultra-high-frequency trading
best_when: You're building algorithmic trading strategies and want a commission-free, developer-friendly API for stocks and crypto.
avoid_when: You need multi-broker routing, advanced options strategies, or institutional-grade execution quality.

auth.notes: API key and secret key pair passed in headers (APCA-API-KEY-ID and APCA-API-SECRET-KEY). Separate paper and live trading credentials.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured JSON errors with code and message; order rejection reasons well-documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Client order ID (client_order_id) enables idempotent order placement. Cancel is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Paper and live endpoints have different base URLs - easy to mix up
Market data has separate subscription tiers (free vs paid feed); free tier has 15-min delay
Order fills are asynchronous - must poll or use WebSocket for fill confirmation
Fractional shares have different minimum amounts than whole share orders
PDT rule (Pattern Day Trader) applies to accounts under $25k - agents must track day trades

security_score_components.security_notes: FINRA/SEC regulated. API key + secret in headers. No scope granularity - trading and account access on same key. client_order_id provides idempotency. Separate paper/live credentials reduce live trading risk.

name: Alpha Vantage
what_it_does: Financial data API providing historical stock prices, real-time quotes, fundamental data, forex, cryptocurrency, and technical indicators (50+ indicators) for quantitative research and trading applications.
use_cases: Historical stock price data for backtesting and model training
Real-time intraday quotes for trading signal generation
Fundamental data (EPS, P/E, revenue) for fundamental analysis agents
Forex and cryptocurrency exchange rates for FX/crypto agents
Technical indicator computation (MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands) without local calculation
not_for: Institutional-grade market data with full tick data or options chains
Ultra-low-latency real-time feeds
News sentiment or alternative data
best_when: An agent or researcher needs comprehensive stock, forex, and crypto data with technical indicators at low/no cost for research and non-HFT applications.
avoid_when: You need premium quality market data, low latency, or institutional-grade coverage.

auth.notes: API key as query parameter (?apikey=). Free and premium keys same mechanism.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages returned as JSON but can be terse; rate limit errors don't always use HTTP 429
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only historical/quote data; fully idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier is only 25 requests/day - far too low for production agent use
Rate limit errors sometimes returned as 200 with error in JSON body, not HTTP 429
API key is in query parameter - appears in server access logs
Data quality inconsistencies for older historical data and smaller cap stocks
Function parameter naming is inconsistent across endpoint families

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in query parameter is a security concern - appears in logs and browser history. HTTPS enforced. No scope granularity. Simple service with minimal security complexity.

name: Amadeus Travel API
what_it_does: Global travel distribution system API providing flight search, booking, hotel rates, points of interest, and travel analytics, backed by the world's largest travel technology company.
use_cases: Searching flight availability and fare quotes for travel booking agents
Booking and managing flight reservations programmatically
Hotel rate search and availability across major chains
Points of interest lookup for travel recommendation agents
Travel delay prediction and trip analytics
not_for: Consumer travel booking without a licensed travel agent relationship
Real-time ancillary service pricing (seat upgrades, baggage) in all cases
Ground transportation or cruise booking
best_when: You're building a travel agent that needs access to global flight inventory, hotel rates, and travel analytics from GDS-quality data.
avoid_when: You need a simple flight search without GDS integration complexity or commercial travel agency licensing.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow. Access token expires in 1799 seconds. Separate test and production environments with different credentials.

paid_tiers.name: Production
paid_tiers.price: per API call, varies by endpoint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured JSON errors with codes and descriptions; travel domain errors can be complex
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search endpoints are idempotent. Booking creation is not.
known_agent_gotchas: Test environment uses synthetic data that doesn't reflect real inventory or pricing
OAuth token must be refreshed before expiry (1799 seconds)
Production access requires additional approval and commercial relationship
Flight price offers expire quickly - booking must follow search immediately
IATA codes required for airports and airlines - agents must have lookup capability

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 client credentials is appropriate for server-to-server. PCI-DSS compliant for booking flows. GDPR compliant. Separate test/prod credentials reduce risk. Token expiry enforced.

name: Amplitude API
what_it_does: Product analytics platform API for event ingestion, user property management, behavioral cohort analysis, and data export, enabling agents to track user behavior and retrieve analytics insights.
use_cases: Tracking user events and properties in AI-powered applications
Querying behavioral analytics to inform agent decision-making
Exporting user cohorts for personalization workflows
Monitoring product metrics and conversion funnels programmatically
Building event-driven automation based on user behavior patterns
not_for: Real-time operational metrics (use Datadog or Grafana)
Log management and infrastructure monitoring
High-frequency event streaming (Amplitude has ingest limits)
best_when: An agent needs to track user interactions in a product and retrieve behavioral analytics for personalization or decision support.
avoid_when: You need real-time operational monitoring, log management, or very high-frequency event ingestion.

auth.notes: API key (for ingest) and secret key pair (for query/export). Different keys for HTTP API vs Dashboard Chart API. Keys via HTTP Basic Auth or headers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes; event validation errors can be silent (events dropped, not rejected)
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Event ingest: same event_id prevents duplicates. Query endpoints are read-only.
known_agent_gotchas: Invalid events can be silently dropped rather than returning an error - hard to debug
Different API keys for ingest vs query - easy to use wrong key
Cohort export is asynchronous - must poll for completion
Data in Amplitude may be 30-60 minutes stale for query endpoints
Dashboard Chart API is separate from HTTP API and uses different auth format

security_score_components.security_notes: HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR compliant. EU data residency option. API keys via HTTP Basic Auth. No scope granularity - ingest key vs query key is the only split. Silent event dropping is a data integrity concern.

name: Android MCP
what_it_does: MCP server for Android device control and automation, enabling AI agents to interact with Android UI elements, capture screenshots, execute adb commands, and automate mobile app testing and interaction.
use_cases: Automating Android app UI testing through AI agent control
Remote Android device interaction via MCP-compatible AI assistants
Mobile app accessibility testing with AI-driven interaction
Capturing and analyzing Android screen state for agent decision-making
Executing adb shell commands and reading device state
not_for: iOS device automation (Android only)
Production app deployments or shipping code
Unrooted devices where adb access is restricted
best_when: You need an AI agent to control or test Android devices/emulators via MCP with natural language instructions.
avoid_when: You need iOS support, or your device doesn't have adb access enabled.

auth.notes: No authentication - requires physical or emulated Android device with adb access. Local network or USB connection.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ADB errors passed through; MCP-level errors are minimal
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Screenshot capture is idempotent. UI interactions (clicks, typing) are not.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires adb enabled on device (developer options) - not available by default
UI element coordinates change with screen resolution and density
App state must be managed manually - agent must know which app is in foreground
ADB connection can drop - agents need reconnection logic
Screenshots include sensitive information from device screen - handle with care

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication on MCP server is a significant risk - anyone on the local network can control the device. ADB access gives near-root level control over the Android device. USB connection is safer than network ADB.
